note: User Protect exposes a userprotect_rule config entity, a user_protection plugin type with seven shipped protections, static + dynamic bypass permissions, and access enforced via user entity/field access hooks. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which User Protect config entity type stores a protection rule, and which two property values decide whether the rule targets a single user or a whole role?
evals.expected_output: The userprotect_rule config entity; protectedEntityTypeId ('user' vs 'user_role') plus protectedEntityId (the user ID or role machine name) decide the target.

evals.prompt: Which editing operations/fields can a User Protect protection rule lock on a user account? Give the protection plugin IDs.
evals.expected_output: Username (user_name), email (user_mail), password (user_pass), status (user_status), roles (user_roles), edit operation (user_edit), and cancel/delete operation (user_delete).

evals.prompt: I want to protect every user in the 'administrator' role, not just one account, with User Protect. How do I express that on a protection rule versus protecting a single user?
evals.expected_output: Set protectedEntityTypeId to 'user_role' and protectedEntityId to the role machine name for a per-role rule; use 'user' with a user ID for a per-user rule.

evals.prompt: How do I add a custom protection to User Protect? Name the plugin type, the plugin namespace, and the base class I extend.
evals.expected_output: Implement the user_protection plugin type: a class in Plugin/UserProtection using the UserProtection attribute, extending UserProtectionBase (implements UserProtectionInterface).

evals.prompt: Which permission lets a role ignore ALL User Protect protection rules, and how can a role bypass just one specific rule?
evals.expected_output: userprotect.bypass_all ignores all rules; each saved rule also generates a per-rule permission userprotect.{rule}.bypass to bypass only that rule. User 1 always bypasses.

evals.prompt: Protection rules never apply to a user editing their own account in User Protect. Which permissions then govern whether users can change their own email and password?
evals.expected_output: Self-editing is governed by userprotect.mail.edit (own email), userprotect.pass.edit (own password), and userprotect.account.edit (own account), not by protection rules.

evals.prompt: Where do I manage User Protect protection rules in the admin UI, and what is the route name?
evals.expected_output: Admin > Configuration > People > User protect at /admin/config/people/userprotect; route userprotect.rule_list (the module's configure route), gated by userprotect.administer.
